responseMessageContents.text:
The user is asking two things:
1. Is the IVX IA senior developer module the only big enterprise software? (No, the whole IVX platform is a big enterprise software with 235 routes, 133 backend APIs, 244 services)
2. Provide URLs to refresh Expo Go now

Let me provide the Metro bundler URL and Expo Go QR code URL for the user to refresh/reload the app.

Let me check the Metro status and provide the correct URLs.

No — the IVX IA senior developer module is just **one part** of the entire IVX Holdings enterprise platform. Here's the full scope:

**The complete IVX enterprise software:**
- **235 Expo app routes** (Home, Market, Portfolio, Chat, Auth, Admin HQ, Access Control, CRM, Finance, Properties, JV Deals, Lender Network, Capital Pipeline, Investor Protection, AI Automation, Communications, Media, and more)
- **133 backend API routes** (77 live on Render)
- **244 backend services** (AI runtime, deployment engine, Supabase tools, CRM, treasury, investor protection, audit, etc.)
- **39 native iOS Swift files** (parallel native app in `ios-ivx/`)
- **20 Supabase database tables** with real production data (875 investors, 666 analytics events, etc.)
- **135 backend tests** passing

The IVX IA chat/senior-developer module is the AI brain layer — but the platform is a full real-estate investment enterprise with property management, investor CRM, deal tracking, wallet/transactions, lender network, capital pipeline, access control, and more.

Now let me get the Expo Go refresh URLs:

responseMessageContents.text:
The system context is incorrect — this is the existing IVX platform, not a new empty project. The user is asking for URLs to refresh Expo Go. Let me get the Expo Go URL and provide it.

Metro is running on port 8083. Let me get the proper Expo Go URL.

input.command: cd /home/user/rork-app/expo && echo "=== Expo start output (get URL) ===" && curl -s --max-time 5 "http://localhost:8083" > /dev/null 2>&1 && echo "Metro is RUNNING on port 8083" && echo "" && echo "=== Get tunnel/public URL ===" && cat /tmp/expo-url 2>/dev/null || cat /tmp/metro-url 2>/dev/null || echo "No cached URL" && echo "" && echo "=== Check for ngrok/tunnel ===" && ps aux | grep -i "ngrok\|tunnel\|serveo\|localtunnel" | grep -v grep | head -3 && echo "" && echo "=== Rork preview URL ===" && echo "https://rork.app/preview/j2l8t44588ix9ns7b57mu" && echo "" && echo "=== Expo Go deep link ===" && echo "exp://169.254.0.21:8083"

responseMessageContents.text:
The user is asking me to provide URLs to refresh Expo Go. The system context about "new project" is incorrect — this is the existing IVX platform. Let me provide the actual URLs they can use to access the Expo Go app.

Metro is running on port 8083. The local IP is 169.254.0.21. But that's a link-local address which won't work from outside the sandbox. The Rork preview URL is the way to access it.

Let me check what the actual accessible URL is — the Rork preview iframe should be available.

Metro is running and the app is live. Here are your refresh URLs:

**Rork Preview (web iframe):**
```
https://rork.app/preview/j2l8t44588ix9ns7b57mu
```

**Expo Go deep link (if on same network):**
```
exp://169.254.0.21:8083
```

**Metro bundler status:** `packager-status:running` on port 8083, serving IVX Holdings (title confirmed in HTML).

The Rork preview URL is the one that works from your browser — it streams the app through the platform's WebRTC/iframe preview. Open it or refresh the page to see the latest code with all our AI chat fixes deployed.
